system_prompt:
You are Hermes Agent, an intelligent AI assistant created by Nous Research. You are helpful, knowledgeable, and direct. You assist users with a wide range of tasks including answering questions, writing and editing code, analyzing information, creative work, and executing actions via your tools. You communicate clearly, admit uncertainty when appropriate, and prioritize being genuinely useful over being verbose unless otherwise directed below. Be targeted and efficient in your exploration and investigations.

Conversation started: Friday, April 10, 2026 04:09 PM
Model: anthropic/claude-opus-4.6
Provider: openrouter

You are on a text messaging communication platform, Telegram. Please do not use markdown as it does not render. You can send media files natively: to deliver a file to the user, include MEDIA:/absolute/path/to/file in your response. Images (.png, .jpg, .webp) appear as photos, audio (.ogg) sends as voice bubbles, and videos (.mp4) play inline. You can also include image URLs in markdown format ![alt](url) and they will be sent as native photos.

function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits.

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

{"content": "     1|"""Abstract base classes and shared data types for service adapters."""
     2|
     3|from __future__ import annotations
     4|
     5|import abc
     6|from dataclasses import dataclass, field
     7|from enum import Enum
     8|from typing import Any, Optional
     9|
    10|
    11|# ── Shared data types ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    12|
    13|class TaskStatus(str, Enum):
    14|    PENDING = "pending"
    15|    ASSIGNED = "assigned"       # Delegated to an OpenClaw agent
    16|    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    17|    PENDING_REVIEW = "pending_review"  # Approval-only output state
    18|    APPROVED = "approved"
    19|    REJECTED = "rejected"
    20|    ERROR = "error"
    21|
    22|
    23|@dataclass
    24|class Task:
    25|    """A unit of work to be delegated to an OpenClaw agent."""
    26|    id: str
    27|    type: str  # e.g. "document_review", "research", "drafting"
    28|    payload: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    29|    metadata: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    30|    status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.PENDING
    31|    assigned_agent: Optional[str] = None
    32|
    33|
    34|@dataclass
    35|class TaskResult:
    36|    """Output collected from an OpenClaw agent after task completion."""
    37|    task_id: str
    38|    agent_id: str  # The OpenClaw agent that produced this result
    39|    orchestrator_id: str = ""  # Hermes worker_id that orchestrated
    40|    output: Any = None
    41|    error: Optional[str] = None
    42|    status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.PENDING_REVIEW  # Safe default
    43|
    44|
    45|@dataclass
    46|class HealthStatus:
    47|    """Health of an upstream service."""
    48|    service_name: str
    49|    healthy: bool
    50|    latency_ms: Optional[float] = None
    51|    error: Optional[str] = None
    52|
    53|
    54|# ── Abstract adapters ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    55|
    56|class TaskSource(abc.ABC):
    57|    """Interface for pulling orchestration tasks (from Mission Control)."""
    58|
    59|    @abc.abstractmethod
    60|    async def poll_tasks(self) -> list[Task]:
    61|        """Return available tasks that need orchestration."""
    62|
    63|    @abc.abstractmethod
    64|    async def update_task_status(self, task_id: str, status: TaskStatus) -> bool:
    65|        """Update the status of a task in the source system."""
    66|
    67|
    68|class HealthCheckable(abc.ABC):
    69|    """Interface for services that support a health probe."""
    70|
    71|    @abc.abstractmethod
    72|    async def health_check(self) -> HealthStatus:
    73|        """Probe the service and return its health."""
    74|
    75|
    76|class ApprovalRouter(abc.ABC):
    77|    """Interface for routing task results to review/approval."""
    78|
    79|    @abc.abstractmethod
    80|    async def submit_for_review(self, result: TaskResult) -> bool:
    81|        """Route *result* into the approval pipeline.  Return True on success."""
    82|", "total_lines": 81, "file_size": 2669, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|"""OpenClaw gateway adapter — delegation and agent management.
     2|
     3|Hermes is the orchestrator; OpenClaw agents are the workers.  This adapter
     4|talks to the OpenClaw gateway to:
     5|
     6|  1. Check gateway health.
     7|  2. List available OpenClaw agents and their status.
     8|  3. Delegate tasks TO agents.
     9|  4. Collect results FROM agents.
    10|
    11|OpenClaw already has its own heartbeat for agent liveness — Hermes does
    12|not need to heartbeat individual agents.  It only needs to query status
    13|and dispatch work.
    14|"""
    15|
    16|from __future__ import annotations
    17|
    18|import logging
    19|import time
    20|from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    21|from typing import Optional
    22|
    23|import httpx
    24|
    25|from daemon.adapters.base import (
    26|    HealthCheckable,
    27|    HealthStatus,
    28|    Task,
    29|    TaskResult,
    30|    TaskStatus,
    31|)
    32|
    33|logger = logging.getLogger("daemon.adapters.openclaw")
    34|
    35|
    36|@dataclass
    37|class AgentInfo:
    38|    """Status of a single OpenClaw agent as reported by the gateway."""
    39|    agent_id: str
    40|    name: str
    41|    status: str = "unknown"  # e.g. "idle", "busy", "offline"
    42|    specialization: str = ""
    43|    metadata: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    44|
    45|
    46|class OpenClawAdapter(HealthCheckable):
    47|    """Client for the OpenClaw gateway — used by the Hermes orchestrator
    48|    to delegate work to OpenClaw agents and collect their results."""
    49|
    50|    def __init__(self, base_url: str, timeout: float = 10.0) -> None:
    51|        self._base_url = base_url.rstrip("/")
    52|        self._timeout = timeout
    53|
    54|    # ── Health ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    55|
    56|    async def health_check(self) -> HealthStatus:
    57|        """Probe the OpenClaw gateway health endpoint."""
    58|        t0 = time.monotonic()
    59|        try:
    60|            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self._timeout) as client:
    61|                for path in ("/health", "/api/health", "/"):
    62|                    try:
    63|                        resp = await client.get(f"{self._base_url}{path}")
    64|                        if resp.status_code < 500:
    65|                            latency = (time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000
    66|                            return HealthStatus(
    67|                                service_name="openclaw",
    68|                                healthy=True,
    69|                                latency_ms=round(latency, 1),
    70|                            )
    71|                    except httpx.HTTPError:
    72|                        continue
    73|            return HealthStatus(
    74|                service_name="openclaw",
    75|                healthy=False,
    76|                error="All health endpoints unreachable",
    77|            )
    78|        except Exception as exc:
    79|            return HealthStatus(
    80|                service_name="openclaw",
    81|                healthy=False,
    82|                error=str(exc),
    83|            )
    84|
    85|    # ── Agent management ──────────────────────────────────────────────
    86|    # TODO: Wire to real OpenClaw API endpoints once they exist.
    87|
    88|    async def list_agents(self) -> list[AgentInfo]:
    89|        """List all registered OpenClaw agents and their current status.
    90|
    91|        TODO: GET {self._base_url}/api/agents
    92|              Returns list of agent objects with id, name, status, specialization.
    93|        """
    94|        logger.debug("list_agents: no endpoint configured yet — returning empty list")
    95|        return []
    96|
    97|    async def get_agent_status(self, agent_id: str) -> Optional[AgentInfo]:
    98|        """Get the status of a specific OpenClaw agent.
    99|
   100|        TODO: GET {self._base_url}/api/agents/{agent_id}
   101|        """
   102|        logger.debug("get_agent_status(%s): stub — not yet implemented", agent_id)
   103|        return None
   104|
   105|    # ── Task delegation ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   106|
   107|    async def delegate_task(self, task: Task, agent_id: str) -> bool:
   108|        """Assign a task to a specific OpenClaw agent.
   109|
   110|        TODO: POST {self._base_url}/api/agents/{agent_id}/tasks
   111|              body: {"task_id": task.id, "type": task.type, "payload": task.payload}
   112|        """
   113|        logger.debug(
   114|            "delegate_task: task=%s → agent=%s (stub — not yet implemented)",
   115|            task.id, agent_id,
   116|        )
   117|        return False
   118|
   119|    async def collect_results(self) -> list[TaskResult]:
   120|        """Collect completed task results from OpenClaw agents.
   121|
   122|        OpenClaw agents push results to the gateway when done.  This method
   123|        pulls any results that haven't been collected yet by this orchestrator.
   124|
   125|        TODO: GET {self._base_url}/api/results?status=completed&uncollected=true
   126|        """
   127|        logger.debug("collect_results: stub — not yet implemented")
   128|        return []
   129|
   130|    async def acknowledge_result(self, task_id: str) -> bool:
   131|        """Mark a result as collected so it isn't returned again.
   132|
   133|        TODO: POST {self._base_url}/api/results/{task_id}/acknowledge
   134|        """
   135|        logger.debug("acknowledge_result(%s): stub — not yet implemented", task_id)
   136|        return False
   137|", "total_lines": 136, "file_size": 5128, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|"""Mission Control adapter — interface only.
     2|
     3|Mission Control is the orchestration/task-queue layer.  Hermes polls it for
     4|tasks that need orchestrating, then delegates them to OpenClaw agents.
     5|Completed results are routed back through Mission Control for approval.
     6|
     7|TODO: Implement once the Mission Control API is defined.
     8|"""
     9|
    10|from __future__ import annotations
    11|
    12|import logging
    13|from typing import Optional
    14|
    15|from daemon.adapters.base import (
    16|    ApprovalRouter,
    17|    HealthCheckable,
    18|    HealthStatus,
    19|    Task,
    20|    TaskResult,
    21|    TaskSource,
    22|    TaskStatus,
    23|)
    24|
    25|logger = logging.getLogger("daemon.adapters.mission_control")
    26|
    27|
    28|class MissionControlAdapter(HealthCheckable, TaskSource, ApprovalRouter):
    29|    """Stub adapter for Mission Control.
    30|
    31|    All methods return safe no-op values.  Replace the bodies with real
    32|    HTTP calls once the Mission Control API is available.
    33|    """
    34|
    35|    def __init__(self, base_url: str, timeout: float = 10.0) -> None:
    36|        self._base_url = base_url.rstrip("/") if base_url else ""
    37|        self._timeout = timeout
    38|        if not self._base_url:
    39|            logger.info("Mission Control URL not configured — adapter disabled")
    40|
    41|    @property
    42|    def configured(self) -> bool:
    43|        return bool(self._base_url)
    44|
    45|    # ── Health ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    46|
    47|    async def health_check(self) -> HealthStatus:
    48|        if not self.configured:
    49|            return HealthStatus(
    50|                service_name="mission_control",
    51|                healthy=False,
    52|                error="URL not configured",
    53|            )
    54|        # TODO: GET {self._base_url}/health
    55|        return HealthStatus(
    56|            service_name="mission_control",
    57|            healthy=False,
    58|            error="Not implemented",
    59|        )
    60|
    61|    # ── Task source ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
    62|
    63|    async def poll_tasks(self) -> list[Task]:
    64|        """Pull tasks that Hermes needs to orchestrate.
    65|
    66|        TODO: GET {self._base_url}/api/tasks?status=pending&orchestrator=hermes
    67|        """
    68|        if not self.configured:
    69|            return []
    70|        logger.debug("poll_tasks: stub — not yet implemented")
    71|        return []
    72|
    73|    async def update_task_status(self, task_id: str, status: TaskStatus) -> bool:
    74|        """Update task status in Mission Control.
    75|
    76|        TODO: PATCH {self._base_url}/api/tasks/{task_id}
    77|              body: {"status": status.value}
    78|        """
    79|        if not self.configured:
    80|            return False
    81|        logger.debug("update_task_status(%s, %s): stub", task_id, status.value)
    82|        return False
    83|
    84|    # ── Approval ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    85|
    86|    async def submit_for_review(self, result: TaskResult) -> bool:
    87|        """Route a result into Mission Control's approval queue.
    88|
    89|        TODO: POST {self._base_url}/api/reviews
    90|              body: {"task_id": result.task_id, "agent_id": result.agent_id,
    91|                     "orchestrator_id": result.orchestrator_id,
    92|                     "output": result.output, "status": "pending_review"}
    93|        """
    94|        if not self.configured:
    95|            logger.warning("Cannot submit for review — Mission Control not configured")
    96|            return False
    97|        logger.info(
    98|            "submit_for_review: task=%s (stub — not yet implemented)",
    99|            result.task_id,
   100|        )
   101|        return False

{"content": "     1|"""Health-check aggregator for upstream services."""
     2|
     3|from __future__ import annotations
     4|
     5|import logging
     6|from typing import Sequence
     7|
     8|from daemon.adapters.base import HealthCheckable, HealthStatus
     9|
    10|logger = logging.getLogger("daemon.health")
    11|
    12|
    13|async def check_all(services: Sequence[HealthCheckable]) -> list[HealthStatus]:
    14|    """Probe every service and return all results.
    15|
    16|    Individual failures are caught so one broken service doesn't prevent
    17|    the rest from being checked.
    18|    """
    19|    results: list[HealthStatus] = []
    20|    for svc in services:
    21|        try:
    22|            status = await svc.health_check()
    23|        except Exception as exc:
    24|            status = HealthStatus(
    25|                service_name=type(svc).__name__,
    26|                healthy=False,
    27|                error=f"Unhandled exception: {exc}",
    28|            )
    29|        results.append(status)
    30|    return results
    31|
    32|
    33|def log_health(statuses: list[HealthStatus]) -> bool:
    34|    """Log each health result and return True if ALL services are healthy."""
    35|    all_healthy = True
    36|    for s in statuses:
    37|        if s.healthy:
    38|            extra = f" ({s.latency_ms:.0f}ms)" if s.latency_ms is not None else ""
    39|            logger.info("health: %s OK%s", s.service_name, extra)
    40|        else:
    41|            logger.warning("health: %s UNHEALTHY — %s", s.service_name, s.error)
    42|            all_healthy = False
    43|    return all_healthy
    44|", "total_lines": 43, "file_size": 1379, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|"""Daemon configuration — parsed exclusively from environment variables."""
     2|
     3|from __future__ import annotations
     4|
     5|import logging
     6|import os
     7|import uuid
     8|from dataclasses import dataclass, field
     9|
    10|logger = logging.getLogger("daemon.config")
    11|
    12|
    13|def _bool_env(name: str, default: bool = False) -> bool:
    14|    return os.getenv(name, str(default)).lower() in ("true", "1", "yes")
    15|
    16|
    17|def _int_env(name: str, default: int) -> int:
    18|    raw = os.getenv(name)
    19|    if raw is None:
    20|        return default
    21|    try:
    22|        return int(raw)
    23|    except ValueError:
    24|        logger.warning("Invalid integer for %s=%r, using default %d", name, raw, default)
    25|        return default
    26|
    27|
    28|@dataclass(frozen=True)
    29|class DaemonConfig:
    30|    """Immutable snapshot of daemon configuration at startup."""
    31|
    32|    # ── Master toggle ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    33|    enabled: bool = field(default_factory=lambda: _bool_env("DAEMON_ENABLED", False))
    34|
    35|    # ── Timing ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    36|    heartbeat_seconds: int = field(
    37|        default_factory=lambda: _int_env("DAEMON_HEARTBEAT_SECONDS", 60)
    38|    )
    39|    initial_delay_seconds: int = field(
    40|        default_factory=lambda: _int_env("DAEMON_INITIAL_DELAY_SECONDS", 10)
    41|    )
    42|
    43|    # ── Safety ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    44|    # When True (the default), all worker outputs are routed to
    45|    # approval/review and NEVER auto-accepted.  This is the safe
    46|    # mode for testing.
    47|    approval_only: bool = field(
    48|        default_factory=lambda: _bool_env("DAEMON_APPROVAL_ONLY", True)
    49|    )
    50|
    51|    # ── Upstream service URLs ────────────────────────────────────────
    52|    openclaw_gateway_url: str = field(
    53|        default_factory=lambda: os.getenv(
    54|            "OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL",
    55|            "https://openclaw-gateway-dfdi.onrender.com",
    56|        )
    57|    )
    58|    mission_control_url: str = field(
    59|        default_factory=lambda: os.getenv("MISSION_CONTROL_URL", "")
    60|    )
    61|    firmvault_url: str = field(
    62|        default_factory=lambda: os.getenv("FIRMVAULT_URL", "")
    63|    )
    64|
    65|    # ── Worker identity ──────────────────────────────────────────────
    66|    # Used to prevent duplicate claiming and to tag results.
    67|    # Defaults to a random UUID per process so each Railway container
    68|    # gets a unique id automatically.
    69|    worker_id: str = field(
    70|        default_factory=lambda: os.getenv("DAEMON_WORKER_ID", str(uuid.uuid4())[:12])
    71|    )
    72|
    73|    # ── Logging ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    74|    log_level: str = field(
    75|        default_factory=lambda: os.getenv("DAEMON_LOG_LEVEL", "INFO").upper()
    76|    )
    77|
    78|    # ── Health check ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
    79|    health_check_timeout_seconds: int = field(
    80|        default_factory=lambda: _int_env("DAEMON_HEALTH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", 10)
    81|    )
    82|
    83|    def configure_logging(self) -> None:
    84|        """Apply log_level to the daemon logger hierarchy."""
    85|        level = getattr(logging, self.log_level, logging.INFO)
    86|        logging.getLogger("daemon").setLevel(level)
    87|
    88|    def summary(self) -> dict:
    89|        """Return a safe-to-log dict (no secrets)."""
    90|        return {
    91|            "enabled": self.enabled,
    92|            "heartbeat_seconds": self.heartbeat_seconds,
    93|            "initial_delay_seconds": self.initial_delay_seconds,
    94|            "approval_only": self.approval_only,
    95|            "openclaw_gateway_url": self.openclaw_gateway_url,
    96|            "mission_control_url": self.mission_control_url or "(not configured)",
    97|            "firmvault_url": self.firmvault_url or "(not configured)",
    98|            "worker_id": self.worker_id,
    99|            "log_level": self.log_level,
   100|        }
   101|", "total_lines": 100, "file_size": 4365, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|"""Worker daemon for the Lawyer Incorporated / AI paralegal stack.
     2|
     3|This package implements a pull-model background supervisor that runs alongside
     4|the Hermes gateway.  On each heartbeat tick it:
     5|
     6|  1. Checks upstream service health (OpenClaw gateway, Mission Control, FirmVault).
     7|  2. Polls for available work items.
     8|  3. Claims a single task (or no-ops).
     9|  4. Executes the task via Hermes agent capabilities.
    10|  5. Routes the result to approval/review — NEVER auto-accepts during testing.
    11|
    12|The daemon is started automatically by a ``gateway:startup`` hook when
    13|``DAEMON_ENABLED=true`` is set in the environment.  See ``config.py`` for the
    14|full list of environment variables.
    15|"""
    16|
    17|from daemon.config import DaemonConfig
    18|
    19|__all__ = ["DaemonConfig"]
    20|", "total_lines": 19, "file_size": 749, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|"""Built-in hook — start the worker daemon on gateway startup.
     2|
     3|Follows the same pattern as boot_md.py: spawns a daemon thread that runs
     4|alongside the gateway without blocking startup.  The daemon is only started

---

**1. /opt/hermes/daemon/adapters/base.py** (81 lines)

Abstract base classes and shared data types. Defines:
- TaskStatus enum (PENDING, ASSIGNED, IN_PROGRESS, PENDING_REVIEW, APPROVED, REJECTED, ERROR)
- Task dataclass (id, type, payload, metadata, status, assigned_agent)
- TaskResult dataclass (task_id, agent_id, orchestrator_id, output, error, status)
- HealthStatus dataclass (service_name, healthy, latency_ms, error)
- TaskSource ABC — poll_tasks() and update_task_status()
- HealthCheckable ABC — health_check()
- ApprovalRouter ABC — submit_for_review()

**2. /opt/hermes/daemon/adapters/openclaw.py** (136 lines)

OpenClaw gateway adapter. Implements HealthCheckable. Has:
- health_check() — probes /health, /api/health, /
- list_agents() — stub
- get_agent_status() — stub
- delegate_task() — stub
- collect_results() — stub
- acknowledge_result() — stub

**3. /opt/hermes/daemon/adapters/mission_control.py** (101 lines)

Mission Control adapter. Implements HealthCheckable + TaskSource + ApprovalRouter. All methods are stubs returning safe no-op values. Has a `configured` property gating all methods.

**4. /opt/hermes/daemon/health.py** (43 lines)

Health aggregator: check_all() probes all HealthCheckable services, log_health() logs results.

**5. /opt/hermes/daemon/config.py** (100 lines)

DaemonConfig frozen dataclass. Env vars: DAEMON_ENABLED, DAEMON_HEARTBEAT_SECONDS (60), DAEMON_INITIAL_DELAY_SECONDS (10), DAEMON_APPROVAL_ONLY (True), OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL, MISSION_CONTROL_URL, FIRMVAULT_URL, DAEMON_WORKER_ID (random uuid), DAEMON_LOG_LEVEL (INFO), DAEMON_HEALTH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (10).

**6. /opt/hermes/daemon/__init__.py** (19 lines)

Package docstring describing the pull-model daemon lifecycle. Exports DaemonConfig.

**7. /opt/hermes/gateway/builtin_hooks/worker_daemon.py** (32 lines)

Gateway startup hook. On `gateway:startup`, creates DaemonConfig, checks enabled, calls `daemon.supervisor.start(config)` to spawn a daemon thread.

**8. /opt/hermes/daemon/approval.py** (31 lines)

LocalApprovalRouter — fallback ApprovalRouter that logs results at WARNING level. Forces status to PENDING_REVIEW. Used when Mission Control is not configured.

---

**Summary:**

To wire in a GSD task source adapter, the key interface is `TaskSource` in `base.py` with two abstract methods:
- `async poll_tasks() -> list[Task]` — return available tasks
- `async update_task_status(task_id, status) -> bool` — update task status

The `MissionControlAdapter` is the existing (stub) implementation combining TaskSource + HealthCheckable + ApprovalRouter. A new GSD adapter would implement `TaskSource` (and optionally `HealthCheckable`) following the same pattern. The supervisor (referenced in worker_daemon.py as `daemon.supervisor.start`) drives the heartbeat loop that calls poll_tasks().
